diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The neuropathology demonstrated by the patient was consistent with three concurrent conditions: achondroplasia, alcohol abuse, and chronic trauma changes. The brain showed no external evidence of contusions but demonstrated abnormalities centred on the midline structures, including fenestration of the septum pellucidum, atrophy of the corpus callosum, and neurofibrillary changes in neurones, all consistent with the pathological changes of dementia pugilistica. The absence of cortical contusions typical of closed head injuries in alcoholics who fall frequently suggested that the dementia pugilistica was likely acquired as a result of occupational trauma while working in the circus or as a missile in 'dwarf-throwing'.
